summary: Design for issue #151: extract the duplicated RequestParam write-field mapping (objectKey + writeMode/ttlSecond/existence/cacheType) in src/datasystem/client/kv_cache/kv_client.cpp into one file-local static function template, and route the five byte-identical write sites (Create, MCreate, Set(key,val,setParam), MSetTx, MSet) through it. Pure refactor with zero change to logged values; the keyless Set(val,setParam) overload is deliberately excluded because it omits the existence field. Helper has internal linkage (no header/ABI change).

ledger.what: Place the mapping helper as a file-local (static/anonymous-namespace) function template inside kv_client.cpp rather than as a shared declaration in common/log/access_recorder.h.
ledger.why: The mapping depends on public KV types (SetParam/MSetParam); exposing it in access_recorder.h would add a layering dependency from common/log onto the public KV API, and the issue scopes the change to kv_client.cpp. A file-local helper matches the existing ReadParamToString convention in this file.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose a file-local, internally-linked function template defined near the top of kv_client.cpp, before its first use.

blast_radius.why: Internal linkage, single translation unit, no ABI or header change.

reversibility.why: Promoting to a shared header helper later is mechanical.

ledger.revisit_if: The same block must also be deduped in java_api/pybind_api, which would justify a shared header helper.

ledger.what: The keyless Set(val, setParam) overload (lines ~242-245) sets only four fields and omits reqParam.existence, unlike every other write site.
ledger.why: Applying the helper there would newly populate existence in that access-record log line, an observable behaviour change that conflicts with the issue's preserve-behaviour mandate.
ledger.what_i_did: Excluded that overload from the dedup; left its four inline assignments unchanged and documented the exclusion.

blast_radius.why: Affects only one overload's access-record log line.

reversibility.why: Folding it in is a one-line addition if the omission is confirmed a bug.

ledger.revisit_if: An owner confirms the existence omission in Set(val, setParam) is a bug rather than intentional.

ledger.what: Apply the helper to all five sites that produce a byte-identical field set (Create, MCreate, Set(key,val,setParam), MSetTx, MSet), not only the two lines named in the issue.
ledger.why: The issue's requested change says to update all call sites; these five produce identical field strings, so including them removes the duplication fully with zero behaviour change.
ledger.what_i_did: Scoped the five identical sites and excluded the divergent Set(val,setParam) overload (see L2).

blast_radius.why: Same translation unit, pure textual substitution with identical output.

reversibility.why: Reverting any site to inline assignments is trivial.

ledger.revisit_if: A reviewer wants the change minimized to exactly the two named locations.

ledger.what: SetParam and MSetParam both expose writeMode (WriteMode), ttlSecond (uint32_t), existence (ExistenceOpt), cacheType (CacheType) with matching names and types, so one function template serves both.
ledger.why: The helper is a template parameterized on the write-param type and accesses those four fields.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified against include/datasystem/kv_client.h (SetParam lines 48-53, MSetParam lines 56-62); confirmed identical field names and types.

blast_radius.why: Compile-time template instantiation; a mismatch would fail to build, not misbehave.

reversibility.why: The template can be specialized or split if the structs diverge.

ledger.revisit_if: Either struct's mapped field set diverges in the future.

ledger.what: Do not add a new dedicated unit test; verify via diff review plus compile plus existing worker-backed system tests.
ledger.why: These paths are covered only by system tests (not cheap units); the helper has internal linkage and is not externally callable; the extraction reproduces each expression verbatim, so a diff review proves equivalence.
ledger.what_i_did: Planned verification as line-by-line equivalence review, build of the translation unit, and existing ST (e.g. embedded_client_test).

blast_radius.why: Affects test-coverage breadth, not runtime behaviour.

reversibility.why: The helper can be promoted to a testable internally-linked form if coverage is required.

ledger.revisit_if: A reviewer requires explicit unit coverage of the mapping.

ledger.what: The original block casts writeMode/existence/cacheType via static_cast<int> but passes ttlSecond directly to std::to_string with no cast; the helper must reproduce this asymmetry exactly.
ledger.why: Any deviation (e.g. casting ttlSecond) could change the produced string and break behaviour equivalence.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified ttlSecond is uint32_t (has a std::to_string overload) and the other three are enums; the helper keeps the same cast/no-cast pattern.

blast_radius.why: Single-field formatting concern within one helper.

reversibility.why: The helper body is one edit to correct if the pattern changes.
